sop.title: People's Representative (PR) SOP Flow Chart
sop.subtitle: Please read the complete SOP flow chart carefully
sop.disclaimer: People Representative(PR) should not take any decision without people's verdict received through polling. If any decision is made without people's verdict or variation to this verdict, it shall be appropriately justified with proper documentation.
sop.agreeCheckbox: I have read and understood the People's Representative SOP Flow Chart and agree to follow it

flowChart.pooling: Pooling / collecting details on problems, needs and demands from the people with urgent, moderate and low priority and collect the ideas and suggestions of Subject Matters Expert separately.
flowChart.segregation: Segregation of issues / ideas with the expert's advice

actions.rewards: Rewards & Recognitions for good and implementable ideas

flowChart.planning: PLANNING — Project (Budget with Activity Based Costing) & Work completion - Time schedules
flowChart.execution: Execution of Work by PR with complete transparency authenticated by visual documentary evidence for presenting final report to the people
flowChart.performanceAppraisal: Polling will be conducted every six months to evaluate the people's representative
flowChart.positiveAction: Continue the good work and prepare for the next evaluation poll after six months.
flowChart.negativeAction: If more than 50% of citizens reject the work, improvements must be made and re-evaluation conducted within 3 months. If rejected again, resignation from the position
flowChart.blockchainNote: Ultimate aim is to bring AI and Blockchain technology in all stages of Governance for complete transparency.
flowChart.electionNote: If the aspirant you selected gets elected in the actual election, this SOP will become live through the app and they will function according to it.

interview.title: The Service Worker's Interview (Aspirant Questionnaire)
interview.subtitle: Please provide clear written responses to the following 9 questions. Your answers will be stored permanently on the portal as a record of your commitment.

questions.q1: Identity & Background: Who are you, what is your educational/professional background, and what specific skills do you bring to manage our ward?
questions.q2: The Resignation Pledge: Are you ready to sign a legal affidavit stating you will resign within 24 hours if the 6-month voter satisfaction poll falls below 50%?
questions.q3: No High Command: If your party (if any) gives an order that contradicts the digital vote of the ward citizens, which one will you follow?
questions.q4: Technical Competence: How will you ensure that every project budget (ABC Costing) is verified by the Expert Portal before it reaches the citizens for a poll?
questions.q5: Transparency: Do you agree to upload every single bill, receipt, and work-progress photo to the 'Live Ledger' within 24 hours of payment?
questions.q6: Emergency Protocol: How will you prove to the citizens that an 'emergency decision' was truly unavoidable and not a way to bypass our voting system?
questions.q7: Expert Consultation: Will you commit to consulting at least three registered experts from our portal for projects exceeding a budget of ₹1 Lakh?
questions.q8: Voter Feedback: How will you handle a situation where the citizens 'Correct' or 'Reject' a plan you have proposed?
questions.q9: The Primary Rule: If you lose in our internal 'Voter Primary Poll,' will you immediately withdraw your nomination and support the person we have chosen?

declaration.title: Declaration & Agreement
declaration.subtitle: Please read and agree to the following declaration
declaration.instruction: Agree to the declaration & SOP, provide signature to continue

checkbox.agreed: The Citizens who elected me are my employers who pay my salary and have appointed me to work.As per this SOP (Standard Operating Procedure), I will work according to the wishes of the citizens and will undergo an evaluation (polling) of my performance every 6 months throughout the 5-year term through this site/app.If more than 50% of the citizens express dissatisfaction with my work and I fail the evaluation, I will improve my performance within the next 3 months and undergo another evaluation through this site/app.If again more than 50% of the citizens reject my performance, I will resign from my position.
checkbox.agreed1: I voluntarily declare that if I receive fewer votes than another aspirant, I will support the aspirant who receives the highest votes and will not contest in the real election. I understand that this platform does not restrict anyone from contesting elections.
checkbox.agreed2: I register as an aspirant with my consent. I agree to share my contact details and be responsible for all my interactions.
checkbox.agreeLabel: I have read all the above details and I agree to them.

election.title: Accountability
election.body: Every decision is tied to verified voter feedback and public accountability.

mission.title: Responsibility
mission.body: Ward-first leadership that owns outcomes and delivers measurable results.

issues.title: Transparency
issues.body: Open processes, visible progress, and public reporting at every step.

foundation.title: Core Values That Define Us
foundation.subtitle: Three pillars that guide every decision, action, and commitment we make to the people of Karnataka.

item1.title: People-Centric
item1.body: Governance designed around citizen needs, not political convenience.

item2.title: Democratic Selection
item2.body: aspirants chosen through transparent, ward-level processes.

item3.title: Verified Citizens
item3.body: Every participant authenticated through EPIC verification.

item4.title: Unique Identity
item4.body: No duplicate entries, no manipulation, pure democracy.

journey.title: History & Milestones
journey.subtitle: The evolution of a people-first political movement.

2017.title: Prajakeeya Vision Announced
2017.body: The foundational philosophy of people-centric governance was publicly articulated, setting the stage for a new kind of political framework.

2018.title: Party Registered with ART Framework
2018.body: Prajaakeeya was officially registered on September 18, 2018, with the Accountability, Responsibility, and Transparency (ART) framework.

2019.title: Lok Sabha Elections Contested
2019.body: Contested the 2019 Lok Sabha elections across Karnataka with citizen aspirants and a non-celebrity policy.

about.title: What is Prajaakeeya?
about.summary: Prajaakeeya is a Karnataka-based political movement focused on people-centric governance. It promotes transparent candidate selection, accountable leadership, and citizen participation anchored in verified electoral data.
about.history1: Announced in 2017 by Upendra with the Prajaakeeya vision of people-first administration.
about.history2: Registered as Prajaakeeya in 2018 with the ART framework: Accountability, Responsibility, Transparency.
about.history3: Contested the 2019 Lok Sabha elections across Karnataka with citizen aspirants and a non-celebrity policy.
about.compareBody: Prajaakeeya foregrounds citizen participation and local accountability, in contrast to top-down party politics (Rajakeeya).
about.notesBody: The party emphasises neutral, data-driven campaigning and community platforms rather than promotional visuals.

governance.title: Governance & Candidate Model
governance.body: Prajaakeeya follows an open candidature model with transparent selection, background checks, and a focus on local accountability. Centralised party funding is discouraged to ensure independence.

points: Open candidature with selection tests and interviews
Background and integrity checks for aspirants
No central party funds; focus on local accountability

performance.title: Electoral Performance
performance.body: Prajaakeeya contested the 2019 Lok Sabha elections and has engaged in local contests, emphasising technology-first campaigning and civic participation.

about.footerNote: This page provides an informational overview of Prajaakeeya. Content is neutral and presented for clarity.

core.title: Core Principles
core.subtitle: The non-negotiable foundations of Prajaakeeya governance.

item1.title: Voter-centric party
item1.body: Citizens are the leaders, and the system is driven by their choices.

item2.title: Cashless model
item2.body: No party fund collections; essential expenses are borne by the party president.

item3.title: No workers/cadre or regional offices
item3.body: A structural departure from traditional party organization.

item4.title: SOP-led selection and governance
item4.body: A defined SOP guides how representatives are chosen and how they function.

sopFlow.title: People’s Representative (PR) SOP Flow
sopFlow.coreRule: All the citizens together should select a candidate and send them to the election. Once that candidate is elected and becomes a public representative, they must come to this app and website and perform their duties according to the SOP (Standard Operating Procedure).

step2.title: Collecting issues and ideas
items: Pooling details on problems, needs and demands with urgent, moderate and low priority.
Collecting ideas and suggestions of intelligent people separately.
Segregation of issues/ideas with experts’ advice.
Rewards & recognitions for good and implementable ideas.

step3.title: Presenting and executing through the system

step4.title: Projects and planning
items: Project types: ongoing projects and new projects.
Planning: project budget (activity-based costing) & time schedules.
Plans: Plan A, Plan B, Plan C, Plan D.

step5.title: Polling and public verdict
step5.body: Polling for public opinion → Public verdict → Plan B.

step6.title: Transparent execution and reporting
items: Execution by PR with complete transparency, authenticated by visual documentary evidence for final reporting.
PR should submit polling details (visual documents of work) and people’s verdict to the UPP party.

step7.title: Performance appraisal and outcomes
items: PR gets one or maximum two more chances to improve; if unsuccessful, he should resign.

step8.title: Governance tech vision
step8.body: Ultimate aim: bring blockchain technology to all stages of governance for complete transparency.

footer.title: Prajaakeeya
footer.subtitle: People-led ward selection with verified voter rolls and transparent outcomes.

landing.ideologyBody: People-first governance, data-backed decisions, and accountable leadership built from the ward up. This platform makes candidate selection transparent and grounded in the real electorate.

sop1.title: Verify Citizens
sop1.body: Import official electoral rolls to anchor every vote to a real EPIC.

sop2.title: Register Aspirants
sop2.body: Citizens declare their intent and publish a manifesto for their ward.

sop3.title: Approve & Publish
sop3.body: Admin review ensures credible aspirants and clean ward lists.

sop4.title: Vote & Declare
sop4.body: Verified ward citizens cast a single vote; results are transparent.

feature1.title: Real roll verification
feature1.body: EPIC validation ensures ward integrity and prevents cross-ward voting.

feature2.title: Single vote guarantee
feature2.body: Each verified voter can cast exactly one ballot in their ward.

feature3.title: Visible outcomes
feature3.body: Aggregated results help the party award the B-Form confidently.

voting.title: Cast Your Vote
voting.subtitle: Select your preferred candidate for your ward
voting.confirmText: Are you sure you want to cast your vote? This action cannot be undone.
voting.successTitle: Thank you for being responsible citizens and participating in the voting process.
voting.successLine1: Once the voting process is completed, you will be able to see the aspirant who has received the highest number of votes. That aspirant will represent your ward and contest in the actual election.If the aspirant wins the election, we will provide complete instructions on this site/app about how the elected representative should work according to the SOP.
voting.successLine2: Citizens will also be able to guide, monitor, and communicate with the representative through this website/app to ensure that the work is carried out as per the citizens’ expectations.
voting.sopIntro: Please read and acknowledge the following before casting your vote:

1.title: Your Vote is Your Responsibility
1.body: By casting your vote, you acknowledge that you have reviewed the candidate's profile, manifesto, and qualifications. Your vote represents your informed decision.

2.title: Confidentiality & Anonymity
2.body: Your vote is completely anonymous and confidential. No one, including administrators, can trace your vote back to you.

3.title: One Vote Per Citizen
3.body: Each verified citizen is entitled to cast one vote per election. Multiple voting attempts will be automatically blocked.

4.title: Vote is Final
4.body: Once your vote is cast and verified with OTP, it cannot be changed or withdrawn. Please ensure your selection is final before proceeding.

5.title: Transparent & Auditable
5.body: All votes are recorded in a tamper-proof system and can be audited by election observers while maintaining voter anonymity.

sop.warningTitle: ⚠️ Important: By clicking "I Agree & Proceed", you confirm that:
sop.confirm1: You have read and understood the voting SOP
sop.confirm2: You are making an informed decision
sop.confirm3: You will not attempt to vote multiple times
sop.confirm4: You accept that your vote is final once cast

voting.confirmDescription: By casting your vote, you are exercising your democratic right to choose a representative for your ward. Your vote is anonymous and secure. Once submitted, this action cannot be reversed.

oath.title: The Real Prajaakeeya
oath.subtitle: Please read before agreeing
oath.declineButton: No, I'm just a silent spectator
oath.para0: I don’t get demotivated with other’s comments, I don’t get influenced by any parties, leaders, their vocabulary skills, promises, money, rallies, social services, blame games, publicity, protests (I protest only for the Right to Recall, until an amendment is made to the Constitution)
oath.para1: I Want A WORKER Who works according to my demands
oath.para4: From today, I am not a follower who blindly follows leaders.

soldOut.acknowledgment:
That's okay.
You've chosen to remain a silent spectator
soldOut.quote:
"The system survives on our silence.
The door is always open — when you're ready to be the employer."

login.votersCanLoginWithGoogle: Citizens can sign in with Google

Voter registration is currently open only for Bengaluru wards (GBA, BBMP).
It will be opened for citizens in other constituencies soon.

register.leftSubtitle: Sign in to access your dashboard.
register.confirmBody: Do you want to proceed with this VOTER ID and take a selfie for verification?
register.title: Register
register.epicNote: We collect the Voter ID (EPIC) only to allocate the correct ward based on your voting area.
register.welcomeHighlight: I am a revolutionary citizen who has decided to take responsibility.
register.consentText: I confirm that my registration on Prajaakeeya is done voluntarily and with my consent.
register.consentCombined: I voluntarily consent to store my identity for constituency verification and confirm that I have read and accepted the Privacy Policy & Terms.
register.motto: Your Ward. Your Voice. Your Duty.
register.oldSystem1: Once in 5 years, we vote and elect one person.
register.oldSystem2: After that? He / She gets full power for 5 years
register.oldSystem3: Our tax money is in their hands
register.newSystem1: I am not just a voter. I am the employer.
register.newSystem2: Government runs on my tax money.
register.newSystem3: So the representative is my employee. He / She must work according to people's needs.
register.newSystem4: What should I do? Just 5 minutes in a week.
register.newSystem5: Through a mobile app or website, I can:
register.newSystem8: ✔ Warn if work is not proper
register.newSystem10: ✔ Recommend for higher position if good
register.oath2: I will give 5 minutes every week.
register.oath3: I will check how my tax money is used.
register.oath4: I will see whether my representative is working honestly.
register.oath5: I am not a silent spectator.
register.pledgeImportant: Important: Please read carefully before proceeding.
register.pledgeBody:
Until now, once every five years, I used to exercise my right only on one day by casting my vote, and for the remaining five years, I remained a silent spectator.

But,
From today onwards, throughout the entire five years, I will make time to use this website/application to evaluate the qualifications of aspirants and choose who should contest in the elections.

After electing them, through this application or website and its SOP, I will stay continuously connected with my representative, submit my demands, and ensure they work with complete transparency and regularly report their performance.
register.pledgeAgree: I have read and agree to the Voter Commitment
register.registrationInfo:
RAJAKEEYA (Old System)
Once in 5 years,
we vote and elect one person.
After that?
He / She gets full power for 5 years
Our tax money is in their hands
We just watch silently
This is Rajakeeya.

PRAJAAKEEYA (New System)
I am not just a voter.
I am the employer.
Government runs on my tax money.
So the representative is my employee.
He / She must work
according to people's needs.
What should I do?
Just 5 minutes in a week.
Through a mobile app or website, I can:
✔ Select my representative
✔ Check his / her work
✔ Warn if work is not proper
✔ Correct through majority polling
✔ Recommend for higher position if good

Simple Oath
"As a citizen,
I will give 5 minutes every week.
I will check how my tax money is used.
I will see whether my representative is working honestly.
I am not a silent spectator.
I am a responsible citizen."
register.responsibility1: A responsible voter doesn't just vote once in 5 years — he monitors his ward every month.
register.responsibility2: Ask questions, demand transparency, and track where every rupee of public money goes.
register.responsibility3: If a representative fails the people, the Right to Recall must be exercised without fear.
register.responsibility4: Participation is power — attend ward meetings, raise issues, and be part of decisions.
register.responsibility5: In Prajaakeeya, leadership begins with citizens — responsibility starts with you.
register.acceptCheckbox: I have read and accept the above points

register.voterListConsent: I voluntarily register myself in the citizens' list

hero.title: Privacy-Policy Disclaimer
hero.effectiveDate: Effective Date: March 25, 2026 | Last Updated: March 25, 2026
hero.intro1: Welcome to Prajaakeeya, a digital civic engagement platform accessible at www.prajaakeeya.org. Prajaakeeya is designed to empower citizens by enabling transparent interaction between users and aspirants at various levels of governance, including Village, Taluk, Zilla, Ward, Assembly, Parliament, Purasabhe, and Nagarasanhe.
hero.intro2: This Privacy Policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, and how we protect your privacy when you access and use the Prajaakeeya platform. By signing in and using our platform, you agree to the terms outlined in this Privacy Policy.
hero.intro3: Prajaakeeya is committed to minimal data collection and maximum user privacy. We believe that civic participation should not come at the cost of personal data exploitation.

s1.title: 1. Introduction
s1.p1: Welcome to Prajaakeeya, a digital civic engagement platform accessible at www.prajaakeeya.org. Prajaakeeya is designed to empower citizens by enabling transparent interaction between users and aspirants at various levels of governance, including Village, Taluk, Zilla, Ward, Assembly, Parliament, Purasabhe, and Nagarasanhe.
s1.p2: This Privacy Policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, and how we protect your privacy when you access and use the Prajaakeeya platform. By signing in and using our platform, you agree to the terms outlined in this Privacy Policy.
s1.p3: Prajaakeeya is committed to minimal data collection and maximum user privacy. We believe that civic participation should not come at the cost of personal data exploitation.

sub21.title: 2.1 Information Provided During Sign-In
sub21.p1: To access the Prajaakeeya platform, users are required to sign in using their Gmail ID (Google Sign-In). The only information we collect during this process is:
sub21.b1: Gmail email address (used as the user's unique identifier on the platform)
sub21.b2: Display name associated with the Google account

sub22.title: 2.2 Information We Do NOT Collect
sub22.p1: Prajaakeeya is committed to a strict no-surveillance policy. We do not collect, store, or process any of the following:
sub22.b3: Location or geolocation data of the user
sub22.b4: Selfies, photographs, or any biometric data of the user
sub22.b5: Browsing history, cookies for tracking, or behavioural analytics
sub22.b6: Contacts, call logs, SMS, or any data from the user's device
sub22.b7: Financial information such as bank details or payment data

sub23.title: 2.3 Information Provided Voluntarily by Aspirants
sub23.p1: Aspirants who register on the platform may voluntarily provide additional information such as their name, background, qualifications, vision statements, and other details relevant to their civic participation.
sub23.p2: This information is provided at the aspirant's own discretion and is displayed on their public profile within the platform.

s3.title: 3. Use of Information
s3.p1: The limited information collected on the Prajaakeeya platform is used solely for the following purposes:
s3.b1: Authenticating users via Google Sign-In to provide access to the platform
s3.b2: Identifying users within the platform using their email address
s3.b3: Enabling communication and interaction between users and aspirants
s3.b4: Facilitating participation at Village, Taluk, Zilla, Ward, Assembly, Parliament, Purasabhe, and Nagarasanhe levels
s3.b5: Maintaining transparency and accountability within the platform
s3.b6: Preventing misuse of user identities and platform features
s3.b7: Supporting community-driven civic participation and engagement
s3.p2: Prajaakeeya does not sell, rent, trade, or share user data with any third parties for marketing, advertising, or commercial purposes.

s4.title: 4. Village / Taluk / Zilla / Ward / Assembly / Parliament / Purasabhe / Nagarasanhe Based Participation
s4.p1: Prajaakeeya enables users to participate in civic engagement at multiple levels of governance. Users may engage with aspirants and civic discussions organized by:
s4.p2: Users choose their level of participation based on their area of interest and civic engagement goals.
s4.p3: Prajaakeeya provides the platform infrastructure for interaction; it does not assign or mandate participation at any level.

s5.title: 5. Data Security
s5.p1: Prajaakeeya takes the security of user information seriously. We implement the following measures to protect user data:
s5.b1: All data transmission between the user and the platform is encrypted using SSL/TLS protocols
s5.b2: Authentication is handled through Google's secure OAuth 2.0 sign-in mechanism
s5.b3: Access to backend systems and databases is restricted to authorized personnel only
s5.b4: Regular security reviews and updates are conducted to maintain platform integrity
s5.b5: We do not store passwords, as authentication is entirely managed by Google
s5.p2: While we take reasonable measures to protect your information, no method of electronic transmission or storage is 100% secure. Users are encouraged to safeguard their Google account credentials independently.

s6.title: 6. Data Sharing and Third-Party Disclosure
s6.p1: Prajaakeeya does not share user information with third parties except in the following limited circumstances:
s6.b1: When required by law, regulation, or legal process (such as a court order or government request)
s6.b2: To protect the rights, safety, or property of Prajaakeeya, its users, or the public
s6.b3: To enforce the platform's Terms of Service or Privacy Policy
s6.p2: We do not engage in any form of data brokerage, advertising-based data sharing, or profiling of users.

s7.title: 7. User Rights and Controls
s7.p1: As a user of Prajaakeeya, you have the following rights:
s7.b1: Right to Access: You may request information about the data we hold associated with your account
s7.b2: Right to Deletion: You may request deletion of your account and associated data at any time by contacting us
s7.b3: Right to Withdraw: You may stop using the platform at any time; your sign-in session can be terminated by logging out
s7.b4: Right to Correction: If any information displayed on the platform about you is incorrect, you may request a correction
s7.b5: Right to Portability: You may request a copy of your data in a commonly used electronic format
s7.p2: To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at the email address provided in the Contact section of this policy.

s8.title: 8. Reporting Misuse
s8.p1: Prajaakeeya takes platform integrity seriously. Users may report the following types of misuse:
s8.b1: Fraudulent use of user ID (email ID) or impersonation of another user
s8.b2: Misleading aspirant information or false claims made by aspirants
s8.b3: Identity misuse, including unauthorized use of another person's credentials
s8.b4: Inappropriate, abusive, or threatening conduct on the platform
s8.b5: Spam, phishing attempts, or any activity intended to disrupt the platform
s8.p2: Reports will be reviewed by the Prajaakeeya team, and appropriate actions will be taken in accordance with platform policies. Actions may include warnings, temporary suspension, or permanent removal of accounts found to be in violation.

s9.title: 9. Cookies and Tracking Technologies
s9.p1: Prajaakeeya uses only essential cookies required for the basic functioning of the platform, such as maintaining your sign-in session. We do not use:
s9.b2: Third-party analytics tools that track user behavior
s9.b3: Pixel tags, web beacons, or similar tracking technologies
s9.b4: Browser fingerprinting or any device identification techniques
s9.p2: Our approach to cookies is strictly functional, not analytical or commercial.

s10.title: 10. Age Requirement
s10.p1: Prajaakeeya is intended only for eligible users and participants above 18 years of age. We do not knowingly collect or solicit information from individuals under 18 years of age.
s10.p2: If we become aware that a user under the age of 18 has signed in to the platform, we will take steps to remove their account and associated data promptly. If you believe a minor has accessed the platform, please contact us immediately.

s11.title: 11. Changes to This Privacy Policy
s11.p1: Prajaakeeya reserves the right to update or modify this Privacy Policy at any time. Changes will be effective upon posting the revised policy on the platform. Users will be notified of significant changes through a notice on the platform or via their registered email address.
s11.p2: We encourage users to review this Privacy Policy periodically to stay informed about how we protect their information. Continued use of the platform after changes are posted constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

s12.title: 12. Platform Neutrality and Non-Political Affiliation
s12.p1: Prajaakeeya is a digital civic participation platform designed to enable users to interact, discuss civic issues, and evaluate aspirants within their participation in elections and governance processes.
s12.p2: Prajaakeeya is not a political party, political organization, or government authority.
s12.p3: The platform does not support, promote, or endorse any political party, political ideology, or individual aspirant.
s12.p4: Prajaakeeya only provides technology tools that allow users and aspirants to interact transparently.
s12.p5: All decisions, discussions, and opinions expressed on the platform belong solely to the users.
s12.p6: The platform does not influence, direct, or control the civic choices or opinions of its users.
s12.p7: Prajaakeeya maintains strict neutrality and serves as an impartial civic infrastructure.

s13.title: 13. Aspirant Information Responsibility
s13.p1: Information provided by aspirants on the Prajaakeeya platform is submitted voluntarily by the aspirants themselves. Prajaakeeya does not independently verify or guarantee the accuracy, authenticity, or completeness of the information provided by aspirants.
s13.b1: Interact with aspirants directly through the platform
s13.b2: Ask questions and seek clarifications on aspirant profiles and claims
s13.b3: Conduct interviews with aspirants using the platform's communication tools
s13.b4: Evaluate aspirant credibility independently based on their own judgment
s13.b5: Cross-reference aspirant claims with publicly available information
s13.p3: Prajaakeeya acts only as a technology platform facilitating communication between users and aspirants. The platform shall not be held responsible for any incorrect, misleading, or incomplete information submitted by aspirants.

s14.title: 14. Platform Purpose and Limitation
s14.p1: Prajaakeeya is designed as a civic engagement platform and does not conduct official elections. The following limitations apply:
s14.b1: Any selection process conducted on the platform is intended only for community evaluation and engagement purposes.
s14.b2: The results displayed on the platform do not represent official election results and have no legal standing in any government or statutory election process.
s14.b3: Prajaakeeya does not influence or control how users make their decisions.
s14.b4: Users participate on the platform voluntarily and at their own discretion.
s14.b5: The platform is not a substitute for any official electoral process conducted by government authorities.
s14.b6: Prajaakeeya does not claim to replace, replicate, or interfere with any statutory election process.

s15.title: 15. Data Retention
s15.p1: Prajaakeeya retains user information (email address and display name) only for as long as the user maintains an active account on the platform. Upon account deletion:
s15.b1: The user's email address and display name are removed from our systems
s15.b2: Any content or interactions associated with the account may be anonymized or deleted
s15.b3: Aspirant profiles and associated content are removed upon request
s15.p2: We do not retain user data beyond what is necessary for the functioning of the platform and compliance with legal obligations.

s16.title: 16. Governing Law
s16.p1: This Privacy Policy shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India, including the Information Technology Act, 2000, and any applicable data protection regulations. Any disputes arising from this policy shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts in Bengaluru, Karnataka, India.

s17.title: 17. Contact Us
s17.p1: If you have any questions, concerns, or requests regarding this Privacy Policy, or if you wish to exercise your rights as described in this policy, please contact us at:
s17.p2: We will make every reasonable effort to respond to your inquiry within a reasonable timeframe.

hero.title: Terms and Conditions
hero.effectiveDate: Effective Date: March 25, 2026 | Last Updated: March 25, 2026
hero.intro: Welcome to Prajaakeeya, a digital civic engagement platform accessible at www.prajaakeeya.org. These Terms and Conditions ("Terms") govern your access to and use of the Prajaakeeya platform, including all features, tools, content, and services offered through the platform.
hero.affiliation: By signing in and using the Prajaakeeya platform, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms. If you do not agree with any part of these Terms, you must not access or use the platform.

s1.title: 1. Introduction and Acceptance of Terms
s1.p1: Prajaakeeya reserves the right to modify these Terms at any time. Continued use of the platform after changes are posted constitutes your acceptance of the revised Terms.

s2.title: 2. Definitions
s2.p1: For the purposes of these Terms:
s2.b1: "Platform" refers to the Prajaakeeya web application, mobile application, and all associated services accessible at www.prajaakeeya.org
s2.b2: "User" refers to any individual who signs in and accesses the Prajaakeeya platform
s2.b3: "Aspirant" refers to any individual who registers on the platform as a civic participant seeking to engage with users at any governance level
s2.b4: "Content" refers to all text, images, information, data, and other materials posted, submitted, or displayed on the platform by users or aspirants
s2.b5: "We," "Us," "Our" refers to Prajaakeeya and its team
s2.b6: "Governance Levels" refers to Village, Taluk, Zilla, Ward, Assembly, Parliament, Purasabhe, and Nagarasanhe

s3.title: 3. Eligibility
s3.p1: To use the Prajaakeeya platform, you must:
s3.b1: Be at least 18 years of age
s3.b2: Have a valid Gmail account for sign-in purposes
s3.b3: Have the legal capacity to enter into a binding agreement
s3.b4: Not have been previously suspended or removed from the platform for violations of these Terms
s3.p2: By signing in, you represent and warrant that you meet all the above eligibility requirements. Prajaakeeya reserves the right to verify eligibility and deny access to anyone who does not meet these criteria.

s4.title: 4. Account Registration and Sign-In
s4.b1: Access to the Prajaakeeya platform requires signing in using a Gmail ID through Google's OAuth 2.0 authentication
s4.b2: Each user must use their own authentic Gmail account; use of fake, borrowed, or shared accounts is strictly prohibited
s4.b3: You are responsible for maintaining the security of your Google account credentials
s4.b4: You are responsible for all activities that occur under your account on the platform
s4.b5: You must notify Prajaakeeya immediately if you become aware of any unauthorized use of your account
s4.b6: Prajaakeeya is not responsible for any loss or damage arising from your failure to protect your account credentials

s5.title: 5. Platform Purpose and Scope
s5.p1: Prajaakeeya is a digital civic participation platform that enables users to interact with aspirants, discuss civic issues, and evaluate aspirants within their participation in elections and governance processes.
s5.p2: The platform facilitates engagement at the following governance levels:
s5.p3: Prajaakeeya provides only the technology infrastructure for civic engagement. The platform does not conduct official elections, endorse aspirants, or influence user decisions in any manner.

s6.title: 6. Platform Neutrality and Non-Political Affiliation
s6.b1: Prajaakeeya is not a political party, political organization, or government authority
s6.b2: The platform does not support, promote, or endorse any political party, political ideology, or individual aspirant
s6.b3: Prajaakeeya only provides technology tools that allow users and aspirants to interact transparently
s6.b4: All decisions, discussions, and opinions expressed on the platform belong solely to the users
s6.b5: The platform does not influence, direct, or control the civic choices or opinions of its users
s6.b6: Prajaakeeya maintains strict neutrality and serves as an impartial civic infrastructure

s7.title: 7. User Conduct and Responsibilities
s7.p1: By using the Prajaakeeya platform, you agree to the following:

sub1.title: 7.1 You SHALL:
sub1.b1: Use the platform only for lawful civic engagement purposes
sub1.b2: Provide accurate and truthful information in all interactions
sub1.b3: Respect other users, aspirants, and the diversity of opinions on the platform
sub1.b4: Report any misuse, abuse, or violations you encounter on the platform
sub1.b5: Comply with all applicable local, state, and national laws

sub2.title: 7.2 You SHALL NOT:
sub2.b1: Impersonate any person or entity, or falsely represent your affiliation with any person or entity
sub2.b2: Use another person's Gmail ID or account to access the platform
sub2.b3: Post, share, or distribute false, misleading, defamatory, or harmful content
sub2.b4: Engage in hate speech, harassment, bullying, threats, or abusive behavior toward any user or aspirant
sub2.b5: Use the platform for commercial advertising, spam, or unsolicited promotional activities
sub2.b6: Attempt to hack, disrupt, or interfere with the platform's infrastructure, security, or functionality
sub2.b7: Use automated bots, scrapers, or similar tools to access or extract data from the platform
sub2.b8: Manipulate ratings, reviews, or any evaluation mechanism on the platform
sub2.b9: Promote violence, illegal activities, or any activity that violates public order
sub2.b10: Use the platform to spread propaganda for or against any political party or aspirant in a manner that constitutes paid or coordinated campaigning

s8.title: 8. Aspirant Conduct and Responsibilities
s8.p1: Aspirants who register on the Prajaakeeya platform agree to the following additional terms:
s8.b1: All information submitted to the platform must be accurate, truthful, and up to date
s8.b2: Aspirants are solely responsible for the content they post, including biographical details, qualifications, vision statements, and promises
s8.b3: Aspirants shall not misrepresent their qualifications, achievements, or affiliations
s8.b4: Aspirants shall engage respectfully with all users and respond to questions and interviews in good faith
s8.b5: Aspirants shall not use the platform to spread misinformation or make false promises
s8.b6: Aspirants acknowledge that users may independently evaluate and verify the information they provide
s8.b7: Aspirants shall not use the platform for any activity that violates the Representation of the People Act or any other applicable election law

s9.title: 9. Aspirant Information Disclaimer
s9.p1: Information provided by aspirants on the Prajaakeeya platform is submitted voluntarily by the aspirants themselves. Prajaakeeya does not independently verify or guarantee the accuracy, authenticity, or completeness of the information provided by aspirants.
s9.b1: Interact with aspirants directly through the platform
s9.b3: Conduct interviews using the platform's communication tools
s9.b4: Evaluate aspirant credibility independently based on their own judgment
s9.b5: Cross-reference aspirant claims with publicly available information
s9.p3: Prajaakeeya acts only as a technology platform facilitating communication between users and aspirants. The platform shall not be held responsible for any incorrect, misleading, or incomplete information submitted by aspirants.

sub1.title: 10.1 User and Aspirant Content

s11.b1: Prajaakeeya is designed as a civic engagement platform and does not conduct official elections
s11.b2: Any selection process conducted on the platform is intended only for community evaluation and engagement purposes

s11.b4: Prajaakeeya does not influence or control how users make their decisions

s11.b6: The platform is not a substitute for any official electoral process conducted by government authorities
s11.b7: Prajaakeeya does not claim to replace, replicate, or interfere with any statutory election process

s12.title: 12. Reporting Misuse
s12.p1: Prajaakeeya takes platform integrity seriously. Users may report the following types of misuse:
s12.b1: Fraudulent use of user ID (email ID) or impersonation of another user
s12.b2: Misleading aspirant information or false claims made by aspirants
s12.b3: Identity misuse, including unauthorized use of another person's credentials
s12.b4: Inappropriate, abusive, or threatening conduct on the platform
s12.b5: Spam, phishing attempts, or any activity intended to disrupt the platform
s12.p2: Reports will be reviewed by the Prajaakeeya team, and appropriate actions will be taken in accordance with platform policies. Actions may include warnings, temporary suspension, or permanent removal of accounts found to be in violation.

s13.title: 13. Account Suspension and Termination
s13.p1: Prajaakeeya reserves the right to suspend or terminate any user or aspirant account at its sole discretion, without prior notice, for the following reasons:
s13.b1: Violation of any provision of these Terms and Conditions
s13.b2: Engaging in fraudulent, abusive, or unlawful activity on the platform
s13.b4: Posting misleading, defamatory, or harmful content
s13.b5: Attempting to hack, disrupt, or compromise the platform's security

s13.p2: Upon termination, the user's access to the platform will be revoked, and associated data may be deleted in accordance with the Privacy Policy. Prajaakeeya is not liable for any loss or damage resulting from account suspension or termination.

s14.title: 14. Limitation of Liability
s14.p1: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law:
s14.b1: Prajaakeeya provides the platform on an "as is" and "as available" basis, without any warranties of any kind, whether express or implied
s14.b2: Prajaakeeya does not warrant that the platform will be uninterrupted, error-free, secure, or free from viruses or other harmful components

s15.p1: You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Prajaakeeya, its founders, team members, and affiliates from and against any and all claims, damages, losses, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable legal fees) arising out of or related to:

s16.title: 16. Privacy
s16.p1: Your use of the Prajaakeeya platform is also governed by our Privacy Policy, available at www.prajaakeeya.org/privacy-policy. The Privacy Policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, and how we protect your privacy. By using the platform, you consent to the collection and use of information as described in the Privacy Policy.

s17.p1: The Prajaakeeya platform may contain links to third-party websites or services that are not owned or controlled by Prajaakeeya. We are not responsible for the content, privacy policies, or practices of any third-party websites or services. s18.p1: Prajaakeeya shall not be liable for any failure or delay in performing its obligations under these Terms due to circumstances beyond its reasonable control, including but not limited to natural disasters, acts of government, war, terrorism, pandemics, internet or telecommunications failures, power outages, cyberattacks, or any other force majeure event.

s20.p1: These Terms and Conditions, together with the Privacy Policy, constitute the entire agreement between you and Prajaakeeya regarding your use of the platform. These Terms supersede all prior agreements, communications, and understandings, whether written or oral, relating to the subject matter herein.

s21.p1: The failure of Prajaakeeya to enforce any right or provision of these Terms shall not constitute a waiver of such right or provision. Any waiver of any provision of these Terms shall be effective only if in writing and signed by an authorized representative of Prajaakeeya.

s22.title: 22. Governing Law and Jurisdiction
s22.p1: These Terms and Conditions shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India, including the Information Technology Act, 2000, and any applicable regulations. Any disputes arising from these Terms or your use of the platform shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts in Bengaluru, Karnataka, India.

s23.title: 23. Contact Us
s23.p1: If you have any questions, concerns, or requests regarding these Terms and Conditions, please contact us at:
s23.p2: We will make every reasonable effort to respond to your inquiry within a reasonable timeframe.

hero.title: Community Guidelines
hero.affiliation: Effective Date: March 25, 2026 | Last Updated: March 25, 2026

s1.title: 1. Purpose of These Guidelines
s1.p1: Prajaakeeya is a digital civic engagement platform that brings users and aspirants together for transparent, respectful, and meaningful civic participation. These Community Guidelines establish the standards of behavior expected from every participant on the platform.
s1.p2: Our goal is to create a safe, inclusive, and constructive space where users can interact with aspirants, discuss civic issues, ask questions, conduct interviews, and evaluate aspirants — all within an environment of mutual respect and accountability.
s1.p3: Every user and aspirant on Prajaakeeya is expected to read, understand, and follow these guidelines. Violations may result in warnings, temporary suspension, or permanent removal from the platform.

s2.title: 2. Core Principles
s2.p1: Prajaakeeya is built on the following core principles that guide all activity on the platform:
s2.b1: Transparency: All interactions should be honest, open, and in good faith
s2.b2: Respect: Every participant deserves to be treated with dignity regardless of their background, opinions, or civic position
s2.b3: Accountability: Users and aspirants are responsible for the content they post and the conduct they exhibit
s2.b4: Neutrality: The platform does not favor any political party, ideology, or aspirant; all participants are treated equally
s2.b5: Civic Purpose: The platform exists solely for civic engagement; commercial, political campaigning, or personal exploitation is not permitted

ss1.title: 3.1 Be Respectful and Civil
ss1.b1: Treat all users, aspirants, and platform participants with courtesy and respect
ss1.b2: Disagreements are natural in civic discussions; express your views constructively without personal attacks
ss1.b3: Listen to differing opinions with an open mind; civic engagement thrives on diverse perspectives
ss1.b4: Address aspirants and fellow users by their name or appropriate form of address
ss1.b5: Avoid condescending, dismissive, or belittling language

ss2.title: 3.2 Be Honest and Authentic
ss2.b1: Use your genuine Gmail ID to sign in; do not create fake or misleading accounts
ss2.b2: Present your views and opinions honestly; do not pretend to represent organizations or groups you are not affiliated with
ss2.b3: Do not fabricate facts, statistics, or claims during civic discussions
ss2.b4: If sharing information about an aspirant or civic issue, ensure it is accurate to the best of your knowledge
ss2.b5: Correct yourself openly if you realize you have shared inaccurate information

ss3.title: 3.3 Engage Constructively
ss3.b1: Focus discussions on civic issues, governance, community welfare, and aspirant evaluation
ss3.b2: Ask meaningful questions to aspirants; the platform is designed for genuine civic interaction
ss3.b3: Contribute to discussions in a way that adds value and promotes understanding
ss3.b4: Use the interview and interaction tools on the platform for their intended civic purpose
ss3.b5: Provide feedback on aspirants based on your genuine assessment, not personal bias or external influence

ss1.title: 4.1 Be Truthful and Transparent
ss1.b1: Provide accurate information about your background, qualifications, experience, and civic vision
ss1.b2: Do not exaggerate, fabricate, or misrepresent your achievements, affiliations, or capabilities
ss1.b3: Update your profile information promptly if any details change
ss1.b4: Be transparent about your civic goals and intentions when engaging with users
ss1.b5: Acknowledge limitations honestly; users respect aspirants who are genuine

ss2.title: 4.2 Engage Respectfully with Users
ss2.b1: Respond to user questions and interview requests in good faith and with patience
ss2.b2: Treat every user's question as legitimate, regardless of its simplicity or complexity
ss2.b3: Do not dismiss, ignore, or ridicule users for their opinions or questions
ss2.b4: Accept constructive criticism gracefully; civic participation involves accountability
ss2.b5: Maintain professional and respectful communication at all times

ss3.title: 4.3 Respect Platform Neutrality
ss3.b1: Do not use the platform to campaign for or against any political party
ss3.b2: Do not attempt to influence users through paid promotions, coordinated messaging, or manipulation
ss3.b3: Do not recruit users for political rallies, fundraising, or activities outside the platform's civic purpose
ss3.b4: Present your civic vision independently without demeaning other aspirants
ss3.b5: Respect the platform's role as a neutral civic space

s5.title: 5. Prohibited Content and Behavior
s5.p1: The following content and behavior are strictly prohibited on the Prajaakeeya platform. Violations will result in action as described in Section 10.

ss1.title: 5.1 Hate Speech and Discrimination
ss1.b1: Content that promotes hatred, violence, or discrimination based on religion, caste, race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, disability, language, or regional identity
ss1.b2: Derogatory slurs, casteist remarks, communal provocation, or any language intended to demean a group of people
ss1.b3: Content that promotes supremacy of any group over another

ss2.title: 5.2 Harassment and Bullying
ss2.b1: Personal attacks, name-calling, or targeted harassment of any user or aspirant
ss2.b2: Persistent unwanted contact, stalking, or intimidation
ss2.b3: Publicly shaming or humiliating any individual on the platform
ss2.b4: Coordinated attacks or pile-on behavior against a user or aspirant
ss2.b5: Doxxing — sharing private or personal information of any individual without their consent

ss3.title: 5.3 Misinformation and Fake Content
ss3.b1: Deliberately spreading false or misleading information about aspirants, civic issues, or governance matters
ss3.b2: Sharing fabricated documents, altered images, deepfakes, or manipulated media
ss3.b3: Creating fake profiles or impersonating real individuals
ss3.b4: Spreading unverified rumors or conspiracy theories as established facts
ss3.b5: Misattributing statements or positions to aspirants or public figures

ss4.title: 5.4 Spam and Commercial Exploitation
ss4.b1: Posting unsolicited advertisements, promotional content, or commercial offers
ss4.b2: Sending repetitive, irrelevant, or bulk messages to users or aspirants
ss4.b3: Using the platform to promote products, services, businesses, or external ventures
ss4.b4: Posting affiliate links, referral codes, or monetization schemes
ss4.b5: Using bots or automated tools to post content or interact with users

ss5.title: 5.5 Illegal and Harmful Activities
ss5.b1: Content that promotes, encourages, or facilitates illegal activities
ss5.b2: Content that incites violence, terrorism, or harm against individuals or groups
ss5.b3: Content that violates the Indian Penal Code, Information Technology Act, Representation of the People Act, or any other applicable law
ss5.b4: Sharing content related to illegal weapons, drugs, or other prohibited items
ss5.b5: Attempting to hack, breach, or compromise the platform's security infrastructure

ss6.title: 5.6 Sexual and Inappropriate Content
ss6.b1: Sexually explicit, suggestive, or obscene content of any kind
ss6.b2: Non-consensual sharing of intimate images or content
ss6.b3: Sexual harassment, unwelcome advances, or inappropriate personal messages
ss6.b4: Content that sexualizes or exploits minors in any way

ss7.title: 5.7 Manipulation and Bad Faith Conduct
ss7.b1: Creating multiple accounts to manipulate discussions, ratings, or evaluations
ss7.b2: Coordinated inauthentic behavior — groups of accounts acting together to artificially boost or suppress an aspirant
ss7.b3: Bribing, coercing, or incentivizing users to post favorable or unfavorable content about aspirants
ss7.b4: Vote manipulation, rating fraud, or any attempt to game the platform's evaluation mechanisms
ss7.b5: Deliberately disrupting civic discussions through trolling, derailing, or provocative behavior with no constructive intent

s6.title: 6. Interaction and Interview Guidelines
s6.p1: Prajaakeeya provides tools for users to interact with and interview aspirants. The following guidelines govern these interactions:

ss1.title: 6.1 For Users Interacting with Aspirants
ss1.b1: Approach interactions with genuine civic curiosity and the intent to understand the aspirant's vision
ss1.b2: Ask relevant questions about governance, community issues, qualifications, and plans
ss1.b3: Allow aspirants adequate time to respond; not all answers can be immediate
ss1.b4: Avoid asking inappropriate personal questions unrelated to civic participation
ss1.b5: Base your evaluation on the aspirant's responses, conduct, and demonstrated civic commitment
ss1.b6: Do not use interactions as a platform for personal attacks or political campaigning

ss2.title: 6.2 For Aspirants During Interactions
ss2.b1: Be prepared to answer questions about your civic vision, qualifications, and plans
ss2.b2: Respond to all reasonable questions honestly and within a reasonable timeframe
ss2.b3: If you cannot answer a question immediately, acknowledge it and commit to a follow-up
ss2.b4: Do not deflect all questions or avoid accountability; users value transparency
ss2.b5: Treat every interaction as an opportunity to demonstrate your civic commitment
ss2.b6: Do not use interactions to solicit donations, recruit supporters, or promote political parties

ss1.title: 7.1 General Standards
ss1.b1: All content posted on the platform should be relevant to civic engagement, governance, or community welfare
ss1.b2: Content should be written in a language and tone that is accessible and respectful
ss1.b3: If sharing facts or data, provide context and sources where possible
ss1.b4: Opinions are welcome but should be clearly identified as opinions, not stated as facts
ss1.b5: Content should contribute positively to the civic discourse on the platform

ss2.title: 7.2 Multimedia Content
ss2.b1: Images, videos, or documents shared on the platform must be relevant to civic discussions
ss2.b2: Do not share copyrighted material without proper authorization
ss2.b3: Do not share altered, manipulated, or misleading media
ss2.b4: All multimedia content must comply with the prohibited content rules in Section 5
ss2.b5: Do not share content that contains personal or private information of others

s8.title: 8. Participation at Governance Levels
s8.p1: Prajaakeeya enables participation at Village, Taluk, Zilla, Ward, Assembly, Parliament, Purasabhe, and Nagarasanhe levels. The following guidelines apply:
s8.b1: Users should participate at governance levels relevant to their area of interest and civic goals
s8.b2: Engaging at multiple levels is permitted, but users should ensure their participation is genuine and constructive
s8.b3: Do not flood discussions at any governance level with irrelevant content or spam
s8.b4: Respect the local context and community-specific issues at each governance level
s8.b5: Cross-level discussions are encouraged when they contribute to broader civic understanding
s8.b6: Do not attempt to dominate or monopolize discussions at any governance level

s9.title: 9. Privacy and Personal Information
s9.b1: Do not share the personal information of other users or aspirants without their consent
s9.b2: Do not attempt to discover, collect, or store the personal data of other participants
s9.b3: Respect the privacy of all individuals on the platform
s9.b4: Do not take screenshots or record interactions for the purpose of harassment, defamation, or misuse
s9.b5: If you receive private or sensitive information inadvertently, do not share or exploit it
s9.b6: Report any privacy violations to the Prajaakeeya team immediately

s10.title: 10. Enforcement and Consequences
s10.p1: Prajaakeeya enforces these Community Guidelines through a graduated response system. The specific action taken depends on the severity and frequency of the violation.

ss1.title: 10.1 Warning
ss1.p1: Issued for first-time minor violations such as off-topic posting, mildly disrespectful language, or unintentional guideline breaches
ss1.p2: The user or aspirant will be notified of the specific violation and reminded of the relevant guideline

ss2.title: 10.2 Content Removal
ss2.p1: Content that violates these guidelines may be removed without prior notice
ss2.p2: The user or aspirant will be informed of the removal and the reason for it

ss3.title: 10.3 Temporary Suspension
ss3.p1: Issued for repeated minor violations, moderate violations such as harassment, misinformation, or manipulation
ss3.p2: The duration of suspension depends on the severity and may range from 24 hours to 30 days
ss3.p3: During suspension, the user or aspirant cannot access the platform

ss4.title: 10.4 Permanent Removal
ss4.p1: Issued for severe violations such as hate speech, serious threats, illegal activity, identity fraud, or persistent violations after prior warnings
ss4.p2: Permanently removed users or aspirants will lose all access to the platform
ss4.p3: Re-registration using a different account after permanent removal is itself a violation

ss5.title: 10.5 Legal Action
ss5.p1: In cases involving illegal activities, Prajaakeeya reserves the right to report the matter to appropriate law enforcement authorities
ss5.p2: Prajaakeeya may cooperate with legal authorities as required by law

s11.title: 11. Reporting Violations
s11.p1: If you encounter any content or behavior that violates these Community Guidelines, you are encouraged to report it through the platform's reporting mechanism.
s11.b1: Fraudulent use of user ID (email ID) or impersonation
s11.b2: Misleading aspirant information or false claims
s11.b3: Identity misuse or unauthorized account access
s11.b4: Hate speech, harassment, or abusive conduct
s11.b5: Spam, commercial exploitation, or irrelevant content
s11.b6: Misinformation, fake content, or manipulated media
s11.b8: Any other conduct that violates these guidelines
s11.p3: All reports are reviewed by the Prajaakeeya team. We aim to review reports promptly and take appropriate action. The identity of the reporting user will be kept confidential to the extent possible.

s12.title: 12. Appeals
s12.p1: If your account has been suspended or content has been removed and you believe the action was taken in error, you may submit an appeal by contacting us at support@prajaakeeya.org.
s12.b2: A description of the action taken (suspension, content removal, etc.)
s12.b3: Your explanation of why you believe the action was incorrect
s12.p3: Appeals will be reviewed by the Prajaakeeya team, and a decision will be communicated to you within a reasonable timeframe. The decision on the appeal is final.

s13.title: 13. Platform Safety and Security
s13.b1: Do not attempt to access accounts, data, or systems that do not belong to you
s13.b2: Do not probe, scan, or test the vulnerability of the platform's security systems
s13.b3: Do not introduce malware, viruses, or any harmful code into the platform
s13.b4: Report any security vulnerabilities you discover to the Prajaakeeya team immediately rather than exploiting them
s13.b5: Do not attempt to reverse-engineer, decompile, or disassemble any part of the platform

s14.title: 14. Civic Responsibility
s14.p1: Prajaakeeya encourages all participants to embrace their civic responsibility:
s14.b1: Use the platform as a tool for genuine civic engagement and community betterment
s14.b2: Encourage fellow citizens to participate in civic discussions constructively
s14.b3: Hold aspirants accountable through respectful questioning and evaluation
s14.b4: Contribute to building a culture of transparency, accountability, and informed civic participation
s14.b5: Remember that the strength of any civic platform lies in the quality of participation by its community

s15.title: 15. Disclaimer
s15.p1: These Community Guidelines are designed to maintain a healthy and productive civic engagement environment. However:
s15.b1: Prajaakeeya cannot monitor all content and interactions in real time
s15.b2: The platform relies on community reporting to identify violations
s15.b3: Prajaakeeya does not endorse or take responsibility for the views, opinions, or content posted by users or aspirants
s15.b4: The platform's enforcement actions are based on available information and platform policies
s15.b5: These guidelines may be updated from time to time to address new challenges and maintain community standards

s16.title: 16. Contact Us
s16.p1: If you have questions about these Community Guidelines or need to report a violation, please contact us at:
